topics.name: 100 Level: Principles of Accounting & Business Mathematics
topics.description: ACC 101 Principles of Accounting, BUS 102 Business Mathematics, and General Studies.

subtopics.name: ACC 101: Double Entry and Financial Statements
objectives: Apply double-entry principles to record business transactions in journals and ledgers
Prepare trial balance, income statement, and balance sheet for sole traders

subtopics.name: BUS 102: Business Mathematics and Statistics
objectives: Solve interest, depreciation, and ratio problems relevant to business accounting
Apply basic statistical methods such as mean, median, and standard deviation to business data

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Intermediate Accounting, Cost, and Tax
topics.description: ACC 201 Intermediate Financial Accounting, ACC 211 Cost Accounting, ACC 301 Taxation.

subtopics.name: ACC 201: Partnership Accounts and Company Accounts
objectives: Prepare partnership accounts including goodwill, admission, and dissolution
Prepare company financial statements complying with IFRS requirements

subtopics.name: ACC 301: Nigerian Taxation — Personal and Company Tax
objectives: Calculate personal income tax under the Personal Income Tax Act (PITA)
Compute company income tax and education tax under the Companies Income Tax Act (CITA)

topics.name: 400 Level: Auditing, Advanced Financial Accounting & Research
topics.description: ACC 401 Auditing, ACC 411 Advanced Financial Accounting, ACC 499 Research Project.

subtopics.name: ACC 401: Auditing Principles and Practice
objectives: Explain the objectives, types, and ethical principles governing external and internal auditing
Plan and execute an audit engagement including internal control evaluation and sampling

subtopics.name: ACC 499: Research Project
objectives: Conduct original accounting or finance research using scientific methodology
Present findings in a professionally formatted dissertation and defend before FUNAI examiners
